Oh look, another 'accidental' canal blockage that just happens to send oil prices skyrocketing within hours. I'm sure those energy traders had absolutely no advance warning whatsoever.
European car factories grinding to a halt because of missing cup holders and floor mats. Apparently the entire automotive industry runs on the logistical equivalent of a house of cards.

Amazon's two-day shipping promise crumbles faster than a stale cookie, but hey, at least Jeff Bezos can still afford his rocket hobby. Prime members everywhere discovering what 'patience' means for the first time since 2005.

Starbucks using a minor coffee bean hiccup to jack up prices because God forbid their profit margins dip below astronomical. That venti latte now costs more than most people's hourly wage, but sure, blame it on the boats.
Wedding planners acting like the apocalypse has arrived because some roses got delayed a few weeks. Apparently love cannot bloom without imported Dutch tulips and the exact shade of baby's breath.

Local bookstores raising prices on paperback novels like they're dealing in rare manuscripts from the Library of Alexandria. Because nothing says 'support literacy' quite like making books less affordable.

And THIS is what really grinds my gears - Sarah's birthday gift being late is somehow newsworthy, but an eighteen dollar burrito? AN EIGHTEEN DOLLAR BURRITO?! We're talking about beans and rice wrapped in a tortilla, not caviar and gold flakes!
